Land Rover Range Rover 4.2 Supercharged Engine Codes

mainEntity.name: How much does a Land Rover Range Rover 4.2 Supercharged engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned 4.2 Supercharged (428PS) engine typically costs £4,500–£9,000 supply only. Fitting adds £900–£2,500 depending on workshop location. Main dealer new engines are no longer available — specialist reconditioning is the route.

mainEntity.name: Is the Land Rover Range Rover 4.2 Supercharged engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — the Jaguar-derived 4.2 SC is widely regarded as one of the most robust engines fitted to the L322. It's considered more reliable than both the BMW V8 that preceded it and the 5.0 SC that followed. The timing system is durable, and the main issues are age-related supercharger wear rather than catastrophic engine failure.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Land Rover Range Rover 4.2 Supercharged?

acceptedAnswer.text: Supercharger coupler wear (clunking noise on cold start), supercharger bearing wear (rattling at idle), and timing chain tensioner wear at very high mileage (150,000+ miles). The engine is also sensitive to cooling system health — overheating can cause head gasket or liner issues.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Range Rover 4.2 Supercharged engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Apply the 60% Land Rover scrap threshold: if your Range Rover is worth £12,000 and a recon engine is £6,500 (54%), it's borderline — assess the rest of the vehicle carefully. If the vehicle is a tired £8,000 example, the same engine exceeds 60% — get a scrap valuation first. Enthusiasts and collectors often justify the spend due to the rarity of the supercharged V8.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Land Rover Range Rover 4.2 Supercharged engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The engine code is listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. The 4.2 Supercharged uses the 428PS (AJ-V8) — a 4.2-litre supercharged V8 producing 390–400 bhp. Tell us your registration date and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the supercharger coupler, or does the whole unit need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: The coupler itself can be replaced (around £400–£800). A full supercharger rebuild (bearings, seals, coupler, oil) typically costs £800–£2,000 and is significantly cheaper than a complete supercharger replacement (£3,000–£5,000).

replacementCosts.h2: Land Rover Range Rover 4.2 Supercharged Engine Replacement Costs

rows.bestFor: No longer available — specialist reconditioning is the route

replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the only engine generation this badge ever used — the 428PS (AJ-V8 4.2 SC). See the Era Map above for era-specific pricing.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a Range Rover 4.2 Supercharged engine typically takes 15–25 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent). The complex supercharger and V8 layout adds significant labour time compared to simpler engines.

cta.label: → Compare Range Rover 4.2 Supercharged engine prices from UK specialists

problems.title: Supercharger Coupler Wear
problems.symptoms: Clunking, knocking or rattling noise from the engine bay on cold startup, which often disappears when warm. Reduced supercharger performance.
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only; if ignored, the coupler can fail completely.
problems.recommendation: The Eaton M112 supercharger's drive coupler is a known wear item on the 4.2 SC. A cracked or worn coupler causes a characteristic clunking noise on cold start. The repair involves replacing the coupler (and often the snout bearings and seals as a kit), which is significantly cheaper than a full supercharger rebuild. If you hear any rattling or clunking from the supercharger area, have it inspected promptly.

problems.title: Supercharger Bearing Wear
problems.symptoms: Rattling noise at idle, whining or grinding from the supercharger, reduced boost pressure.
problems.recommendation: The supercharger bearings can wear over time, particularly in high-mileage examples. Rattling at idle is the first indication that something is going wrong. A full supercharger rebuild (bearings, seals, coupler, oil) typically costs £800–£2,000 and restores the supercharger to like-new condition. Ignoring bearing wear can lead to catastrophic supercharger failure and potential engine damage from debris.

problems.title: Timing Chain Tensioner Wear (High Mileage)
problems.symptoms: Ticking or rattling noise at low rpm, particularly on cold start.
problems.typicalMileage: 150,000+ miles (much less common than on the 5.0 SC).
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only; if the chain snaps, it's an interference engine — catastrophic failure.

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: at first sign of rattle, book inspection today.

problems.recommendation: Unlike the later 5.0-litre AJ133 which has well-documented timing chain issues at low mileage, the 4.2 SC's timing system is considered durable. The 4.2-litre engines have revised primary and secondary timing chains, guides and tensioners and as such, there is little need to replace them. However, at very high mileage (170,000+ miles), tensioners and guides can show wear. If you hear any ticking or rattling, have the timing system inspected promptly. Regular oil changes with the correct specification are critical to timing chain longevity.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: Range Rover 4.2 Supercharged — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Simple, cost-effective fix — do it preventatively
Full rebuild restores performance — cheaper than replacement
Preventative — catches fault before catastrophic failure
Interference engine — piston-valve contact means little is left to repair
Viable if the rest of the vehicle is solid; often cheaper to fit a good used engine

buyingChecks.title: Buying This 4.2 Supercharged? Check These First
buyIf: * No clunking or rattling from the supercharger on cold start
* Full service history with regular oil changes (every 10,000 miles or annually)
* No history of overheating — these engines need a healthy cooling system
avoidIf: * Clunking noise on cold start — supercharger coupler wear
* Rattling at idle — supercharger bearing wear
* Ticking or rattling from the engine — timing chain tensioner wear
* Overheating or coolant loss — potential head gasket or liner issue

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The 4.2 Supercharged is one of the most robust engines fitted to the L322 Range Rover. It's widely considered more reliable than both the BMW M62 V8 that preceded it and the 5.0 SC that followed. However, it's now a 15–20 year old engine, and supercharger wear is the primary concern. A £6,000 reconditioned engine on a Range Rover valued at £12,000 is 50% of the car's value — at the Land Rover scrap threshold. On a sub-£8,000 example, think carefully before committing. Enthusiasts often justify the spend due to the rarity and desirability of the supercharged V8.

cta.label: → Compare Range Rover 4.2 Supercharged engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Land Rover Range Rover 4.2 Supercharged (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for clunking or rattling (supercharger coupler wear)
* Supercharger Noise — Any whining, grinding, or rattling at idle?
* Service History — Regular oil changes using the correct 5W-30 or 5W-40 spec
* Gearbox Service — ZF 6HP26 serviced every 60,000 miles
* Cooling System — Check coolant level and condition — overheating kills these engines
* Oil Leaks — Check for oil leaks from valve cover gaskets and timing chain covers
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any unresolved issues?
* MOT History — Check for emissions failures and corrosion (rear arches, subframe)
